caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: S2 is biopharma QC, not US independent 541380 testing laboratories.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied role mix is 5-digit 54138 and leaves a 0.5997 residual wage share; the residual allocation is necessarily broad.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Physical robotics and LLM-class AI are distinct; these estimates do not credit physical automation as immediate AI replacement.
inputs.pi_dist: No US 541380 fee-schedule or margin study was found that isolates AI savings pass-through.
inputs.pi_dist: S8 is UK TIC commentary, while S1 is one regulated environmental-testing program; neither establishes an industry-wide US pricing model.
inputs.pi_moat: This scores retention of savings, not terminal demand; the regulatory human/physical floor is addressed separately in terminal demand.
inputs.pi_moat: Accreditation scope, customer concentration, geography, and test category vary materially across the code.
inputs.t50_years: No representative AI-adoption survey for independent US 541380 laboratories was found.
inputs.t50_years: S2 surveys biopharma executives and may overstate digitization capacity relative to lower-middle-market commercial labs.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: The cited survey is adjacent and is not a measurement of the target NAICS or of labor displacement.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Generic business AI-use surveys are not used as a substitute for effective lab-workflow adoption.
inputs.historical_analogs: This is an analogy, not a quantitative causal estimate or a 541380 historical time series.
inputs.historical_analogs: Biopharma QC has different regulation, scale, and capital budgets.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: No extracted Census tabulation for NAICS 541380 and the $1-10M EBITDA band was available in the reviewed source.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: S4 is a broad bank-sponsored owner survey, not an industry ownership census.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: This does not mean a shortage cannot exist; it means it was not documented by the reviewed evidence for this score input.
inputs.active_consolidators: The sources cover distinct subsegments and are not a comprehensive count of all buyers in 541380.
inputs.active_consolidators: A named acquirer is evidence for buyability only, not for AI task replacement or adoption speed.
inputs.buy_mult: S7 is an advisory-marketing source, not a disclosed-deal database.
inputs.buy_mult: Enterprise-value and revenue thresholds do not exactly map to the supplied $1-10M EBITDA target, and lab capex or customer concentration can materially change the multiple.
inputs.exit_mult: S8 is UK TIC coverage; its deal range, currency, legal environment, and mix are not a US small-laboratory comp set.
inputs.exit_mult: No multiple expansion is assumed in capture or terminal-demand inputs; it is confined to this multiple input.
inputs.pricing_structure: The mix across environmental, materials, food, calibration, product safety, and other 541380 labs is unknown.
inputs.pricing_structure: S8 is UK sector commentary and S1 is a specific US lead-testing example, not a full US pricing survey.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $172,295 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541380); class <5: 2,444 firms, avg revenue/firm $373,954 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $58,524 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 961 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,076,685 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $168,501 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 734 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,220,721 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $347,543 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 749 firms, avg revenue/firm $6,303,646 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $986,521 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 187 firms, avg revenue/firm $26,036,572 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $4,074,724 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 151 firms, avg revenue/firm $89,322,291 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $13,978,939 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 187

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Role fractions isolate workflow, data, documentation, scheduling, and report work from the physical test, sample, instrument, technical-review, and accreditation-accountability work. Adjacent QC modernization supports the workflow use cases, while regulated accreditation supports a human/physical floor; neither source directly measures the supplied occupations.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541380 ($17,068,820,469) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541380 ($26,656,582,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics and exact-artifact binding pass. All eight registered sources support the claims attributed to them, and the packet keeps the biopharma, broad-owner, adviser, UK-TIC, and subsegment limitations explicit. The useful conclusion remains supportable, but the numerous authored proxy, scope, and uncertainty caveats require publication with caveats.

sources.title: The National Lead Laboratory Accreditation Program (NLLAP) | US EPA
sources.what_it_supports: An official example of a regulated testing segment: accredited laboratories must demonstrate analytical capability, undergo onsite assessment, and continue proficiency testing; specified lead samples must be analyzed by an EPA-recognized lab.

sources.title: Pharma’s QC lab of the future: Boosting speed, compliance, and quality
sources.what_it_supports: A 2025 survey of 103 biopharma executives reports investment in lab automation, AI, data capture, sample management, and traceability, but also substantial disconnected systems and limited automation. It is an adjacent, more capitalized population.

sources.title: ABS - Characteristics of Business Owners: 2022 Tables (Employer Businesses)
sources.what_it_supports: Census makes an employer-business owner-age table available, establishing the appropriate broad US ownership-demographic source; this packet does not claim a 541380-specific extraction from it.

sources.title: BofA Report: 74% of Small and Mid-Sized Business Owners Expect Revenue to Increase in the Next Year
sources.what_it_supports: A weighted 2025 US owner survey reports that 40% lack a succession plan, but its broad size and industry coverage does not document a testing-laboratory-specific shortage.

sources.title: UES Expands into Indiana with Acquisition of GME Testing
sources.what_it_supports: Direct 2025 evidence of a national buyer acquiring a 23-person firm providing geotechnical engineering, construction-materials testing, and foundation inspection/testing.

sources.title: Intertek strengthens its products testing business with acquisition of leading flooring products testing company
sources.what_it_supports: Direct 2025 evidence of a global TIC buyer acquiring a 26-employee US product-testing laboratory.

sources.title: M&A Advisor for TIC Companies | Aventis Advisors
sources.what_it_supports: A TIC M&A advisory source says smaller regional labs below $25M enterprise value typically trade at 6–8x EBITDA; it is adviser marketing rather than a transaction database.

sources.title: Testing, Inspection and Certification: Why resilience continues to attract consolidation
sources.what_it_supports: UK TIC market commentary reports recurring and regulatory demand, transaction activity, and a disclosed-multiple range of 7.5–12.5x; it is useful directionally but is not a US small-laboratory comp set.

title: Testing Laboratories and Services
